fields.description: Customer-facing name for the device this peer is for (e.g. alice-laptop, ci-runner).
fields.label: Label

fields.description: The tenant whose /48 this peer joins. The client lands inside this tenant's VPC and reaches every VM in it. Immutable after insert — the scope.
fields.label: Tenant

fields.description: The region gateway VM that terminates this peer (denormalized; resolved automatically on insert). Every customer of the region shares this one gateway's single wg0.
fields.label: Gateway

fields.description: The customer's WireGuard public key. The customer keeps its private key; Atlas never sees it. Immutable after insert.
fields.label: Client Public Key

fields.description: The gateway's wg0 public key, read from the gateway at enroll time. One key per gateway, shared by every peer — denormalized here so the config dialog can render without re-SSHing.
fields.label: Gateway Public Key

fields.description: The client's /128 inside the tenant /48 (4th hextet 0x0001), derived from (tenant, peer). The address the laptop carries on its WireGuard interface. Source of truth is atlas.networking.derive_client_address.
fields.label: Client Address

fields.description: The customer-side AllowedIPs: the whole tenant /48 (route the whole VPC out the tunnel). Advisory — the customer may edit it without weakening isolation.
fields.label: Allowed IPs

fields.description: What the customer dials: the gateway's fixed reserved public IPv4 and the shared WireGuard port.
fields.label: Endpoint
